name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value larger than the original input

name: align_result_not_multiple
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_result_not_multiple(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The result of aligning down must itself be a multiple of b; otherwise the alignment is broken

name: aligned_not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_not_idempotent(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: An already-aligned address should be unchanged by align_to_usize; violating this means the two specs are inconsistent

name: align_always_zero
code:
proof fn phi_4_align_always_zero(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) == 0,
{
}
reason: align_to_usize should not collapse every value to zero; for a >= b it must return a positive aligned address

name: align_unit_changes_value
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_unit_changes_value(a: usize)
    requires
        a < usize::MAX,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, 1) != a,
{
}
reason: Every integer is 1-aligned so align_to_usize(a,1) must equal a; failure means aligned and align_to_usize disagree on trivial case
